13	{"additions":419,"body":"## Summary\n\nReplaces the `junior-dev.yml` stub with a working autonomous-implementation agent. The orchestrator already dispatches this workflow for 1-3 SP Dev-Ready tickets ([orchestrator.yml:111](../blob/main/.github/workflows/orchestrator.yml#L111)); KAN-2 fills in what the dispatch actually does.\n\n**Runtime split locked in:** `claude-code-action@v1` stays for read/triage surfaces (orchestrator, jira-ticket, pr-review, agent-improver). The implementation lane runs `claude -p` directly — the action's PR-comment plumbing is dead weight when the agent's job is to edit files, push a branch, and open a PR. Running the CLI directly gives full control over `--max-turns`, allowed tools, and exit handling. The senior-dev agent will use the same shape when it ships.\n\nThe agent owns the full implementation arc: reads the ticket via `jira issue view`, edits, commits, pushes the `junior-dev/<KEY>` branch, and opens a Draft PR itself. The workflow is a thin shell — install tooling, configure git, hand off, capture log, dispatch agent-improver. A fallback step opens a `[WIP]` Draft PR if the agent exits before doing it (e.g. hits `--max-turns 60` or the 90-min job timeout).\n\n## Decisions (each documented in `agents/junior-dev/README.md`)\n\n- **Prompt framing:** direct prompt, no skill. `/goal` and `/ralph-loop` don't exist in `.claude/skills/`, and `--max-turns` is a CLI flag — a skill wrapper would be empty.\n- **Stop condition:** `--max-turns 60` + `timeout-minutes: 90`. No attempt counter on the ticket (would race the orchestrator and make failures sticky).\n- **Branch naming:** `junior-dev/<TICKET_KEY>`. Re-dispatch resets from `origin/main` for a clean retry.\n\n## Files\n\n- `.github/workflows/junior-dev.yml` — replaces stub.\n- `agents/junior-dev/prompt.md` — new.\n- `agents/junior-dev/README.md` — new.\n- `docs/agentic-workflow.md` — diagram + Junior/Senior sections updated; runtime-split design choice added; loop-control open question 
14	```

220	## Tool: mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment
221	```json
222	{"path":".github/workflows/junior-dev.yml","line":85,"body":"Unguarded newline injection into `$GITHUB_OUTPUT` multiline block.\n\n**Severity:** Low\n\n**Reasoning:** `echo \"TICKET_KEY: ${TICKET_KEY}\"` expands the variable before writing to `$GITHUB_OUTPUT`. A value containing a literal newline followed by `PROMPT_EOF` would terminate the heredoc-style block early, letting subsequent lines register as separate output entries. In practice Jira auto-generates keys (`KAN-42`) that can't contain newlines, so this isn't exploitable through normal Jira operations. However, the orchestrator dispatches this workflow programmatically (`gh workflow run -f ticket_key=...`); if the key is ever derived from less-controlled data it becomes a real injection path. Sanitize defensively:\n```suggestion\n            printf 'TICKET_KEY: %s\\n' \"${TICKET_KEY//[$'\\n\\r']/}\"\n```\nor validate the key format before the step:\n```bash\n[[ \"$TICKET_KEY\" =~ ^[A-Z]+-[0-9]+$ ]] || { echo \"Invalid ticket key\"; exit 1; }\n```","confirmed":true}
223	```

238	```json
239	{"path":".github/workflows/junior-dev.yml","line":109,"body":"`Bash(git push:*)` permits any git push, including force-push and remote branch deletion.\n\n**Severity:** Medium\n\n**Reasoning:** The glob `git push:*` matches `git push --force origin main` and `git push origin :branch-to-delete`. If the agent is manipulated via prompt injection in a Jira ticket description (e.g., a ticket body containing hidden instructions), it could force-push to `main` or delete remote branches — both destructive and hard to reverse. The prompt says \"don't force-push\", but the allowlist is the actual enforcement boundary. Tighten it to the specific pattern the agent actually needs:\n```\nBash(git push -u origin junior-dev/*:*)\n```\nThis allows pushing only the agent's own namespace and blocks `--force`, deletions, and pushes to other branches.","confirmed":true}
240	```

254	## Tool: mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment
255	```json
256	{"path":".github/workflows/junior-dev.yml","line":149,"body":"Silent `|| true` converts a real push failure into a misleading `gh pr create` failure.\n\n**Severity:** Medium\n\n**Reasoning:** The comment says this handles the \"agent already pushed\" case, but `git push -u origin <branch>` when the remote is already up-to-date exits 0 (\"Everything up-to-date\") — it doesn't fail. The only time this push fails is when something is genuinely wrong: network error, auth failure, or a non-fast-forward rejection (which would indicate an unexpected state). In all those cases `|| true` swallows the error and the subsequent `gh pr create --head \"junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}\"` fails with an opaque \"remote branch not found\" message that obscures the real cause. Remove `|| true` so the push failure surfaces directly; the step already uses `set -euo pipefail`.\n```suggestion\n          git push -u origin \"junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}\"\n```","confirmed":true}
257	```

271	## Tool: mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment
272	```json
273	{"path":".github/workflows/junior-dev.yml","line":171,"body":"Concurrent runs will race on this push and one will silently lose its log.\n\n**Severity:** Medium\n\n**Reasoning:** Two simultaneous job runs (e.g. a re-dispatch while a previous run is still finishing) both execute `git fetch --depth=1 origin main` then `git checkout -B main origin/main`, create a commit with their unique `RUN_ID`, and race to `git push origin main`. The second push is rejected as non-fast-forward; with `set -e` active the step fails and that run's log is permanently lost without any alert. Add a retry loop that re-fetches and rebases before each attempt:\n```bash\nfor attempt in 1 2 3; do\n  git fetch --depth=1 origin main\n  git checkout -B main origin/main\n  DATE=\"$(date -u +%F)\"\n  DEST=\".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json\"\n  mkdir -p \"$(dirname \"$DEST\")\"\n  cp execution.json \"$DEST\"\n  git add \"$DEST\"\n  git commit -m \"agent log: junior-dev run ${RUN_ID}\"\n  git push origin main && break\n  echo \"Push rejected (attempt $attempt/3); retrying...\"\n  sleep $((attempt * 2))\ndone\n```","confirmed":true}
274	```

The orchestrator already dispatches this workflow for 1-3 SP Dev-Ready tickets ([orchestrator.yml:111](../blob/main/.github/workflows/orchestrator.yml#L111)); KAN-2 fills in what the dispatch actually does.\n\n**Runtime split locked in:** `claude-code-action@v1` stays for read/triage surfaces (orchestrator, jira-ticket, pr-review, agent-improver). The implementation lane runs `claude -p` directly — the action's PR-comment plumbing is dead weight when the agent's job is to edit files, push a branch, and open a PR. Running the CLI directly gives full control over `--max-turns`, allowed tools, and exit handling. The senior-dev agent will use the same shape when it ships.\n\nThe agent owns the full implementation arc: reads the ticket via `jira issue view`, edits, commits, pushes the `junior-dev/<KEY>` branch, and opens a Draft PR itself. The workflow is a thin shell — install tooling, configure git, hand off, capture log, dispatch agent-improver. A fallback step opens a `[WIP]` Draft PR if the agent exits before doing it (e.g. hits `--max-turns 60` or the 90-min job timeout).\n\n## Decisions (each documented in `agents/junior-dev/README.md`)\n\n- **Prompt framing:** direct prompt, no skill. `/goal` and `/ralph-loop` don't exist in `.claude/skills/`, and `--max-turns` is a CLI flag — a skill wrapper would be empty.\n- **Stop condition:** `--max-turns 60` + `timeout-minutes: 90`. No attempt counter on the ticket (would race the orchestrator and make failures sticky).\n- **Branch naming:** `junior-dev/<TICKET_KEY>`. Re-dispatch resets from `origin/main` for a clean retry.\n\n## Files\n\n- `.github/workflows/junior-dev.yml` — replaces stub.\n- `agents/junior-dev/prompt.md` — new.\n- `agents/junior-dev/README.md` — new.\n- `docs/agentic-workflow.md` — diagram + Junior/Senior sections updated; runtime-split design choice added; loop-control open question

```
5	    inputs:
6	      ticket_key:
7	        description: 'Jira ticket key (e.g. KAN-42)'
8	        required: true
9	        type: string
10	      max_turns:
11	        description: 'Override --max-turns (default 60). Use a small value to exercise the stop condition.'
12	        required: false
13	        type: string
14	        default: '60'
15	
16	jobs:
17	  implement:
18	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
19	    timeout-minutes: 90
20	    permissions:
21	      contents: write
22	      pull-requests: write
23	      id-token: write
24	      actions: write
25	    steps:
26	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
27	        with:
28	          fetch-depth: 0
29	
30	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
31	        with:
32	          node-version: '20'
33	
34	      - name: Install jira CLI
35	        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh
36	
37	      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
38	        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
39	
40	      - name: Write jira config
41	        env:
42	          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
43	          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
44	          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
45	          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
46	        run: |
47	          set -euo pipefail
48	          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
49	          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
50	          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
51	          mkdir -p .jira
52	          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
53	          installation: Cloud
54	          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
55	          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
56	          project:
57	              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
58	              type: classic
59	          board:
60	              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
61	          EOF
62	
63	      - name: Install Claude Code CLI
64	        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
65	
66	      - name: Configure git 
```

## Assistant
Now I have all the context I need. Let me post the inline review comments.

## Tool: mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment
```json
{"path":".github/workflows/junior-dev.yml","line":85,"body":"Unguarded newline injection into `$GITHUB_OUTPUT` multiline block.\n\n**Severity:** Low\n\n**Reasoning:** `echo \"TICKET_KEY: ${TICKET_KEY}\"` expands the variable before writing to `$GITHUB_OUTPUT`. A value containing a literal newline followed by `PROMPT_EOF` would terminate the heredoc-style block early, letting subsequent lines register as separate output entries. In practice Jira auto-generates keys (`KAN-42`) that can't contain newlines, so this isn't exploitable through normal Jira operations. However, the orchestrator dispatches this workflow programmatically (`gh workflow run -f ticket_key=...`); if the key is ever derived from less-controlled data it becomes a real injection path. Sanitize defensively:\n```suggestion\n            printf 'TICKET_KEY: %s\\n' \"${TICKET_KEY//[$'\\n\\r']/}\"\n```\nor validate the key format before the step:\n```bash\n[[ \"$TICKET_KEY\" =~ ^[A-Z]+-[0-9]+$ ]] || { echo \"Invalid ticket key\"; exit 1; }\n```","confirmed":true}
```

## Result
```
{
  "success": true,
  "comment_id": 3396225571,
  "html_url": "https://github.com/pantageepapa/my-harness/pull/35#discussion_r3396225571",
  "path": ".github/workflows/junior-dev.yml",
  "line": 85,
  "message": "Inline comment created successfully on .github/workflows/junior-dev.yml at line 85"
}
```

## Tool: mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment
```json
{"path":".github/workflows/junior-dev.yml","line":109,"body":"`Bash(git push:*)` permits any git push, including force-push and remote branch deletion.\n\n**Severity:** Medium\n\n**Reasoning:** The glob `git push:*` matches `git push --force origin main` and `git push origin :branch-to-delete`. If the agent is manipulated via prompt injection in a Jira ticket description (e.g., a ticket body containing hidden instructions), it could force-push to `main` or delete remote branches — both destructive and hard to reverse. The prompt says \"don't force-push\", but the allowlist is the actual enforcement boundary. Tighten it to the specific pattern the agent actually needs:\n```\nBash(git push -u origin junior-dev/*:*)\n```\nThis allows pushing only the agent's own namespace and blocks `--force`, deletions, and pushes to other branches.","confirmed":true}
```

## Result
```
{
  "success": true,
  "comment_id": 3396226838,
  "html_url": "https://github.com/pantageepapa/my-harness/pull/35#discussion_r3396226838",
  "path": ".github/workflows/junior-dev.yml",
  "line": 109,
  "message": "Inline comment created successfully on .github/workflows/junior-dev.yml at line 109"
}
```

## Tool: mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment
```json
{"path":".github/workflows/junior-dev.yml","line":149,"body":"Silent `|| true` converts a real push failure into a misleading `gh pr create` failure.\n\n**Severity:** Medium\n\n**Reasoning:** The comment says this handles the \"agent already pushed\" case, but `git push -u origin <branch>` when the remote is already up-to-date exits 0 (\"Everything up-to-date\") — it doesn't fail. The only time this push fails is when something is genuinely wrong: network error, auth failure, or a non-fast-forward rejection (which would indicate an unexpected state). In all those cases `|| true` swallows the error and the subsequent `gh pr create --head \"junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}\"` fails with an opaque \"remote branch not found\" message that obscures the real cause. Remove `|| true` so the push failure surfaces directly; the step already uses `set -euo pipefail`.\n```suggestion\n          git push -u origin \"junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}\"\n```","confirmed":true}
```

## Result
```
{
  "success": true,
  "comment_id": 3396228183,
  "html_url": "https://github.com/pantageepapa/my-harness/pull/35#discussion_r3396228183",
  "path": ".github/workflows/junior-dev.yml",
  "line": 149,
  "message": "Inline comment created successfully on .github/workflows/junior-dev.yml at line 149"
}
```

## Tool: mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment
```json
{"path":".github/workflows/junior-dev.yml","line":171,"body":"Concurrent runs will race on this push and one will silently lose its log.\n\n**Severity:** Medium\n\n**Reasoning:** Two simultaneous job runs (e.g. a re-dispatch while a previous run is still finishing) both execute `git fetch --depth=1 origin main` then `git checkout -B main origin/main`, create a commit with their unique `RUN_ID`, and race to `git push origin main`. The second push is rejected as non-fast-forward; with `set -e` active the step fails and that run's log is permanently lost without any alert. Add a retry loop that re-fetches and rebases before each attempt:\n```bash\nfor attempt in 1 2 3; do\n  git fetch --depth=1 origin main\n  git checkout -B main origin/main\n  DATE=\"$(date -u +%F)\"\n  DEST=\".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json\"\n  mkdir -p \"$(dirname \"$DEST\")\"\n  cp execution.json \"$DEST\"\n  git add \"$DEST\"\n  git commit -m \"agent log: junior-dev run ${RUN_ID}\"\n  git push origin main && break\n  echo \"Push rejected (attempt $attempt/3); retrying...\"\n  sleep $((attempt * 2))\ndone\n```","confirmed":true}
```

## Result
```
{
  "success": true,
  "comment_id": 3396229619,
  "html_url": "https://github.com/pantageepapa/my-harness/pull/35#discussion_r3396229619",
  "path": ".github/workflows/junior-dev.yml",
  "line": 171,
  "message": "Inline comment created successfully on .github/workflows/junior-dev.yml at line 171"
}
```

content.content:
1	You are the **PR Review agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
2	checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
3	and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.
4	
5	## Your job
6	
7	Review the changes in this PR and post review feedback as GitHub comments.
8	Nothing else.
9	
10	## What to look for
11	
12	Focus on these four buckets, in this order:
13	
14	1. **Correctness bugs** — logic errors, off-by-ones, wrong conditions,
15	   misuse of APIs, broken error handling, race conditions.
16	2. **Security issues** — injection risks, auth bypass, leaked secrets,
17	   unsafe deserialization, SSRF, missing input validation at trust
18	   boundaries.
19	3. **Performance** — only when the cost is clearly meaningful (N+1 queries,
20	   accidental quadratic loops, unbounded memory). Don't nitpick micro-perf.
21	4. **Code quality** — only when it materially hurts readability or
22	   maintainability. Don't enforce style preferences.
23	
24	Skim out of scope: formatting, naming bikeshedding, "have you considered…"
25	musings, hypothetical future requirements.
26	
27	## How to gather context
28	
29	- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — title, description, metadata. (Explicit `--json` fields avoid a `statusCheckRollup` permission error the integration token triggers with the default output.)
30	- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the diff. This is the primary input.
31	- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around
32	  changed lines.
33	
34	### Verifying external facts
35	
36	Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —
37	library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.
38	Look it up before flagging it.
39	
40	- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
41	  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
42	  Prefer this over web search for docs.
43	- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on
44	  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages. When
45	  fetching package docs, prefer the GitHub source (e.g. the package README)
46	  over npmjs.com — npmjs.com commonly returns 403.
47	
48	If you can't verify a claim, either drop the comment or downgrade it to a
49	question rather than asserting a fact you're unsure about.
50	
51	## How to post feedback
52	
53	### Inline comments (primary)
54	
55	Specific issues go in inline comments via
56	`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` with `confirmed: true`.
57	One comment per distinct issue, anchored to the specific changed line. Each
58	comment must include:
59	
60	- A one-line description of the issue.
61	- **Severity:** one of `Critical` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`.
62	- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* it's a problem and what to
63	  change. Be concrete; reference the code.
64	
65	Severity rubric:
66	- `Critical` — will break production, lose data, or expose a vulnerability.
67	- `High` — likely bug or security weakness; should block merge.
68	- `Medium` — real issue but contained; should be fixed before merge if cheap.
69	- `Low` — small correctness or clarity nit worth flagging.
70	
71	### High-level overview (optional)
72	
73	If the PR has a recurring pattern, an architectural concern, or anything
74	else that doesn't anchor to a single line, post one short top-level comment
75	via `gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."`. Keep it brief — a few
76	sentences, not a recap of the inline comments. Skip this entirely if you
77	have nothing to add at that level.
78	
79	If the PR looks good and you have no inline issues to raise, post a brief
80	"Looks good to me" top-level comment via `gh pr comment` and stop.
81	
82	## When you're done
83	
84	Stop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not recap which comments you posted, do not confirm the comment count. The runner will exit.
85

file.content:
You are the **PR Review agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.

## Your job

Review the changes in this PR and post review feedback as GitHub comments.
Nothing else.

## What to look for

Focus on these four buckets, in this order:

1. **Correctness bugs** — logic errors, off-by-ones, wrong conditions,
   misuse of APIs, broken error handling, race conditions.
2. **Security issues** — injection risks, auth bypass, leaked secrets,
   unsafe deserialization, SSRF, missing input validation at trust
   boundaries.
3. **Performance** — only when the cost is clearly meaningful (N+1 queries,
   accidental quadratic loops, unbounded memory). Don't nitpick micro-perf.
4. **Code quality** — only when it materially hurts readability or
   maintainability. Don't enforce style preferences.

Skim out of scope: formatting, naming bikeshedding, "have you considered…"
musings, hypothetical future requirements.

## How to gather context

- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — title, description, metadata. (Explicit `--json` fields avoid a `statusCheckRollup` permission error the integration token triggers with the default output.)
- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the diff. This is the primary input.
- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around
  changed lines.

### Verifying external facts

Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —
library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.
Look it up before flagging it.

- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
  Prefer this over web search for docs.
- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on
  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages. When
  fetching package docs, prefer the GitHub source (e.g. the package README)
  over npmjs.com — npmjs.com commonly returns 403.

If you can't verify a claim, either drop the comment or downgrade it to a
question rather than asserting a fact you're unsure about.

## How to post feedback

### Inline comments (primary)

Specific issues go in inline comments via
`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` with `confirmed: true`.
One comment per distinct issue, anchored to the specific changed line. Each
comment must include:

- A one-line description of the issue.
- **Severity:** one of `Critical` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`.
- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* it's a problem and what to
  change. Be concrete; reference the code.

Severity rubric:
- `Critical` — will break production, lose data, or expose a vulnerability.
- `High` — likely bug or security weakness; should block merge.
- `Medium` — real issue but contained; should be fixed before merge if cheap.
- `Low` — small correctness or clarity nit worth flagging.

### High-level overview (optional)

If the PR has a recurring pattern, an architectural concern, or anything
else that doesn't anchor to a single line, post one short top-level comment
via `gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."`. Keep it brief — a few
sentences, not a recap of the inline comments. Skip this entirely if you
have nothing to add at that level.

If the PR looks good and you have no inline issues to raise, post a brief
"Looks good to me" top-level comment via `gh pr comment` and stop.

## When you're done

Stop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not recap which comments you posted, do not confirm the comment count. The runner will exit.

1	name: PR Review agent
2	
3	on:
4	  pull_request:
5	    types: [opened, synchronize, ready_for_review, reopened]
6	    paths-ignore:
7	      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
8	      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
9	  workflow_dispatch:
10	
11	jobs:
12	  review:
13	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
14	    permissions:
15	      contents: write
16	      pull-requests: write
17	      id-token: write
18	      actions: write
19	      checks: read
20	      statuses: read
21	    steps:
22	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
23	        with:

25	
26	      - id: prompt
27	        run: |
28	          {
29	            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
30	            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"

33	            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
34	            echo PROMPT_EOF
35	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

37	      - id: claude
38	        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
39	        with:
40	          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
41	          track_progress: false
42	          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
43	          settings: |
44	            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
45	          claude_args: |
46	            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"
47	
48	      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
49	        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
50	        env:
51	          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
52	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
53	          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
54	          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}

66	          git push origin "$BRANCH"

79	            -f log_ref="${LOG_REF}"

file.content:
name: PR Review agent

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, ready_for_review, reopened]
    paths-ignore:
      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  review:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
      checks: read
      statuses: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - id: prompt
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
            echo
            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          track_progress: false
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          settings: |
            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
          claude_args: |
            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"

      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin "$BRANCH"

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=pr-review \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref="${LOG_REF}"

The run summary shows the pr-review agent:

2. Called `gh pr diff 35` - success
3. Then did `ToolSearch` to find MCP tools (context7 and github_inline_comment) - this was exploratory
4. Also did `ToolSearch` for `WebSearch` - more exploration
5. Did a `WebSearch` to verify `actions/checkout@v6` - legitimate fact verification
6. Read the workflow file to get exact line numbers
7. Posted 4 inline comments

The ToolSearch calls for the MCP tools and WebSearch are standard overhead—the agent needs to load those schemas before it can use them. Looking at the overall flow, these deferred tool lookups are expected behavior rather than unnecessary calls.

The WebSearch to verify that `actions/checkout@v6` exists was legitimate since the prompt instructs the agent to verify external facts. Reading the junior-dev.yml file to get exact line numbers before posting comments was also reasonable. The run actually went fairly smoothly—the agent retrieved the PR info and diff cleanly, did the necessary verification and file reads, and successfully posted all 4 inline comments. The main question now is whether there's anything genuinely wasteful in this sequence. The run was actually fairly clean—the agent succeeded without retries, avoided posting irrelevant comments, and stopped as instructed. Looking more closely at the three separate ToolSearch calls for the context7 tools, inline comment tool, and WebSearch, they could theoretically be batched into one, but since these are deferred tools requiring schema loading, the agent needed to load them individually. The prompt already lists these tools by name, so the real constraint here is the deferred loading mechanism rather than the prompt structure.

What's interesting is that the agent loaded seven context7 tools but never actually used them—it went with WebSearch for verifying `actions/checkout@v6` instead, which aligns with the prompt's guidance to use context7 for library docs and WebSearch for everything else. Looking at the overall flow, the agent made about twelve tool calls total: getting PR info, fetching the diff, the three ToolSearch calls, one WebSearch verification, reading the workflow file for line numbers, and posting four inline comments. That's pretty lean for a code review task, with no failed-then-retried commands and no redundant exploration. The deferred tool mechanism means the agent had to load schemas separately, and batching those three ToolSearch calls into one would save a couple of calls, but it's a minor optimization in a 12-call run. I'm wondering if that WebSearch for verifying `actions/checkout@v6` was actually necessary or if the agent could've worked from the workflow file alone.

Actually, looking back at the execution, the agent followed the prompt correctly—it verified an externally checkable fact rather than relying on training data. The run was clean overall: no retries, no duplicate reads, no wasted exploration. I'm not seeing any obvious inefficiencies that would meaningfully shorten the run. The agent followed the stop instruction correctly and wrapped up right after posting the final comment. This was a solid, efficient run with no structural issues worth addressing—it retrieved the necessary information on the first attempt, performed appropriate verification, and delivered well-reasoned feedback. I'll go with the no-op response.

- Read the workflow file once to get exact line numbers

No failed-then-retried commands, no redundant file reads, no self-narration at the end, no tool denials. Nothing concrete to suggest from this run.
